item.title: Burt Bacharach
item.description: Era1957–2023GenresPop soulBrill BuildingTraditional popFilm scoreListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify Burt Bacharach is the most harmonically sophisticated songwriter in the history of American pop — the figure who brought Ravel, Debussy, and Brazilian bossa nova into the three-minute single without sacrif...

item.title: The Temptations
item.description: Era1960–present (classic era 1964–1971)GenresSoulPop soulPsychedelic soulSceneDetroit / Motown RecordsListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify The Motown act that best illustrates the label’s capacity for reinvention.

item.title: The Who
item.description: Era1964–present (primary period: 1965–1973)GenresModFreakbeatArt rockHard rockRock operaSceneModShepherd’s BushLondonListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify Most drummers keep time.

item.title: Blues
item.description: Periodc. 1890s–presentNamedc. 1912, with the first published “blues” sheet musicGeographic originAfrican American communities of the U.S.

item.title: BLUES
item.description: The blues began in the Deep South in the 1890s and became the structural and emotional bedrock of nearly everything that followed — jazz, R&B, rock & roll, and rock all draw on its forms.
